description: PaperCut MF server cannot be patched because PaperCut has acknowledged the vulnerability but has not released a fix. PaperCut advisory PC-2025-0034 states fix expected in PaperCut MF v24.1 (release timeline: late January 2026). Current version: v23.0.9.
compensating_controls: 1. Print management server isolated on dedicated VLAN (10.30.20.0/24) with firewall ACL allowing only print queues from corporate endpoints. 2. Admin interface restricted to 2 named admin IPs. 3. Enhanced logging — all print jobs and admin actions logged to Splunk. 4. CrowdStrike Falcon agent with behavioral detection. 5. Daily vulnerability scan for new exploit signatures.
vendor_status: Vendor acknowledged. Fix in PaperCut MF v24.1 (late January 2026). Interim workaround (disable remote admin API) applied per PaperCut advisory.
review_notes: Vendor dependency — Keystone cannot patch until PaperCut releases v24.1. All 5 compensating controls verified operational as of Dec 15 review. No exploit attempts detected in Splunk logs. Exception valid per Section 4.3 (vendor dependency).

description: Legacy ERP system running Windows Server 2019. Microsoft released patch KB5040430 in July 2024. Patch was tested in staging on August 5 and caused ERP application crash (Java runtime conflict). Vendor (SAP) confirmed incompatibility with SAP GUI 8.00 patch level 3. SAP fix expected in SAP GUI 8.00 patch level 5 (release: Q1 2026).
compensating_controls: 1. RDS service disabled — ERP accessed only via Citrix published app. 2. Server isolated on dedicated VLAN with firewall ACL. 3. Windows Firewall rules block all inbound RDP (port 3389). 4. CrowdStrike Falcon with behavioral detection. 5. Network IDS signatures for CVE-2024-38077 deployed.
vendor_status: Microsoft patch available but incompatible with SAP application. SAP fix (GUI PL5) expected Q1 2026.
review_notes: Both Microsoft and SAP involved. Microsoft patch causes SAP crash. Cannot apply OS patch until SAP releases compatible version. All compensating controls verified. Exception reviewed quarterly — last review Nov 20.
